rulings.Case Title: ALFREDO PLASCENCIA VS. ALL SEAS WHOLESALE INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 14, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT ALL SEAS WHOLESALE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies summary judgment. The Court grants and denies summary adjudication in part. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 5th causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendant misclassified Plaintiff as an exempt employee when plaintiff worked as the quality control person. Plaintiff's deposition states that: (1) Plaintiff stopped supervising people (79:12-14); (2) Plaintiff no longer had the authority to order people around (79:25-80:5); (3) the quality control job duties included driving a truck and picking up fish (85:21-86:11), unloading the fish (88:24-89:10), processing seafood orders and doing maintenance (89:24-90:15), and Plaintiff did not supervise any employees (90:16-18) or have input on employee schedules (131:21-23). Defendant's President also admitted that Plaintiff's duties changed to picking up product, loading trucks, and taking care of the shellfish room when he became the quality control person. (Ex. 19 attached to Howse Depo.) The Court denies summary adjudication on the 4th cause of action because Plaintiff demonstrates injury, see Jaimez v. DAIOHS, USA (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 1286, 1306, and Defendant's constitutional/punitive damages cases are inapposite. The Court grants summary adjudication on the 6th cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy cause of action. Labor Code § 132a cannot serve as the predicate policy to support the cause of action as a matter of law. See Dutra v. Mercy Medical Center Mt. Shasta (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 750. The Court denies summary adjudication on the 7th cause of action because a UCL claim is a recognized means to recover unpaid wages. See Cortez v. Puralator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal. 4th 163, 179; Janik v. Rudy, Exelrod & Zieff (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 930, 936. The Court denies summary adjudication on the 8th cause of action. Plaintiff has standing to maintain the claim as an aggrieved employee. Defendant fails to shift the burden of demonstrating that other employees were not harmed by Defendant's conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The Plainitff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST VS. 2400 TELEGRAPH PARTNERSHIP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 14, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JOHN CHAN AS TRUSTEE OF THE SHU PUN CHAN TRUST Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff JOHN CHAN as Trustee of the SHU PUN CHAN TRUST's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses is GRANTED. On or before January 11, 2013, Defendant 2400 TELEGRAPH PARTNERSHIP shall provide substantive, verified responses, without objection to the following discovery: (1) Plaintiff's Requests for Admission to Defendant, Set One; (2) Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories to Defendant, Set One; (3) Plaintiff's Request for Production to Defendant, Set One; and (4) Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories to Defendant, Set One. Plaintiff JOHN CHAN as Trustee of the SHU PUN CHAN TRUST's request for sanctions in the amount of $1,985.00 is GRANTED. Moving party to prepare the Order and submit to the Court at the date of hearing for signature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: VWR INTERNATIONAL, LLC VS. CSC SCIENTIFIC COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 14, 2012, Line 11, PLAINTIFF VWR INTERNATIONAL, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. VWR International, LLC ("VWR") moves for summary judgment, and not summary adjudication. VWR has not its initial burden of proof on summary judgment. In its motion, VWR addresses only the contractual indemnity and declaratory relief causes of action, and not the partial and total equitable indemnity causes of action. "[A] plaintiff bears the burden of persuasion that 'each element of' the 'cause of action' in question has been 'proved,' and hence that 'there is no defense' thereto. (Code Civ. Proc., § 437c, subd. (o)(1).)" (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) Thus, VWR has not its initial burden of persuasion. Further, VWR admits that a triable issue of fact exists as to whether it is entitled to partial or total equitable indemnity. (Motion, P&A, p. 1:16-18, 15:15-16; Reply, p. 1:26 - 2:2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
